Description: Keep interfaces small and focused on a single responsibility. Clients should not depend on methods they don't use
Do: Create multiple focused interfaces rather than one large one
Dont: Create fat interfaces with many methods that most implementers don't need

Description: High-level modules should depend on abstractions not on concrete implementations
Do: Type-hint interfaces in constructors. Use DI container for binding

Description: Use strict type declarations for parameters and return types in all methods
Do: Declare strict_types=1. Type-hint all parameters and return types. Use union and intersection types
Dont: Rely on PHP's implicit type coercion. Leave parameters and returns untyped
Code Good:
<?php declare(strict_types=1);

class UserService {
    public function findById(int $id): ?User {
        return User::find($id);
    }
    public function create(CreateUserDTO $dto): User {
        return User::create($dto->toArray());
    }
    public function search(string $query, int $limit = 10): Collection {
        return User::where('name', 'like', \%{$query}%")->limit($limit)->get();
    }
    public function delete(int $id): void {
        User::findOrFail($id)->delete();
    }
}"

Description: Use interfaces for contracts (what) and abstract classes for shared behavior (how)
Do: Interfaces for capability contracts. Abstract classes when sharing implementation
Dont: Use abstract classes as mere interfaces, or interfaces when you need shared code

Description: Use traits to compose reusable behaviors across unrelated classes
Do: Keep traits focused on single concerns. Use traits for cross-cutting behaviors
Dont: Create God traits with many methods. Use traits to simulate multiple inheritance

Description: Use PHPStan/Psalm template annotations for type-safe generic collections and services
Do: Use @template annotations for reusable type-safe code. PHPStan validates at analysis time
Dont: Use mixed or untyped collections losing type safety

Description: Use readonly properties for immutable value objects and DTOs
Do: Make DTOs and value objects readonly. Use constructor promotion
Dont: Use regular mutable properties for objects that should never change

Description: Use constructor parameter promotion to reduce boilerplate in class definitions
Do: Use promoted parameters for simple property initialization

Description: Use backed enums for type-safe values that have a fixed set of possible states
Do: Use backed enums for status fields. Implement methods on enums for behavior
Dont: Use string constants or plain strings for fixed value sets

Description: Use named arguments for functions with many parameters or boolean flags
Do: Use named arguments for clarity especially with boolean or optional parameters
Dont: Pass positional arguments to functions with many optional parameters

Description: Implement PSR interfaces for framework-agnostic code and interoperability
Do: Use PSR interfaces when building reusable libraries or when framework independence matters
Dont: Create custom interfaces when a PSR interface already exists

Description: Make classes final by default. Only allow extension when explicitly designed for it
Do: Declare classes as final unless they are designed to be extended. Use composition over inheritance
Dont: Leave all classes non-final allowing uncontrolled inheritance

Description: Use typed collections instead of generic arrays for type safety and documentation
Do: Create specific collection classes or use PHPStan generics for arrays

Description: Use intersection types to require a value to implement multiple interfaces simultaneously
Do: Use intersection types when a parameter must satisfy multiple contracts
Dont: Create a single interface that extends all required interfaces just for type hinting

Description: Design APIs where methods return $this for readable method chaining
Do: Return $this or self from builder/configuration methods
Dont: Return void from methods that could benefit from chaining
